release_candidate_required_gates.evidence: go test ./... covers proxy/WebRTC policy, managed extra arg collision, profile lock, dry-run command generation, and wrapper/browser package behavior.
python3 -m unittest tests.test_detector_harness covers target listing, matrix planning, validation, sanitization rejection, operational timeout, and KG JSONL edges.

python3 -m unittest tests.test_package_runtime covers platform plan, missing browser rejection, wrapper/browser zip packaging, checksums, non-placeholder SPDX-2.3-style SBOM metadata, file hashes/sizes, and source/patchset provenance hashes.

BrowseForge commit 76ec639 dispatches browseforge-chromium through the Chromium launcher; local dogfood evidence records GET/runtime config, profile create, session launch, RuntimeID reporting, profile isolation, and Playwright Bind connection.

source-acquisition.json records checked-out Chromium refs/tags/150.0.7871.101, Linux deps sync, Docker deps image, GN output at out/BrowseForgeLinuxDocker, and applied BrowseForge scaffold/webdriver/hardware/screen/platform/timezone/locale/user-agent/storage-quota/plugins/WebRTC/audio/canvas/WebGL/feature-parity/fonts patches.

release_candidate_required_gates.blocker: No patched Chromium binary exists yet; build automation now exposes an explicit Docker deps-image `build-chrome` command for `autoninja -C out/BrowseForgeLinuxDocker chrome`, but the command has not produced a release-grade patched chrome binary/package.

Source-level navigator.webdriver, navigator.hardwareConcurrency, navigator.deviceMemory, Screen API dimension, navigator.platform, timezone, navigator language, navigator.userAgent, navigator.userAgentData, and StorageManager quota and navigator.plugins/pdfViewerEnabled and WebRTC ICE candidate and WebAudio output and Canvas readback, WebGL vendor/renderer, feature parity, and font availability patches are applied in the external Chromium checkout, but no patched Chromium binary or live detector matrix exists yet; release-grade BrowserLeaks/Pixelscan/iphey/BrowserScan/CreepJS/SannySoft evidence remains required.

Packaging now emits non-placeholder SPDX-2.3-style SBOM metadata, input file hashes, source-acquisition/patchset manifest hashes, and git commit provenance for packaged inputs; release-grade SBOM/provenance still requires a real patched Chromium artifact plus Chromium DEPS/source checkout metadata from the release build.
